verdict.summary: gRPC call rejected due to missing or invalid authentication credentials. Token expired, wrong scopes, or misconfigured interceptor.

dead_ends.action: Disable authentication on server for testing
dead_ends.why_fails: Disabling auth in production is a security vulnerability. Use proper test tokens or a test environment.

dead_ends.action: Hardcode credentials in client source code
dead_ends.why_fails: Hardcoded credentials get committed to version control and cannot be rotated. Use environment variables or credential files.

workarounds.action: Attach auth metadata via client interceptor or call credentials
Verify token validity and expiration before sending
workarounds.how: Check token expiry time; refresh OAuth2/JWT tokens before they expire

workarounds.action: Check server-side auth interceptor configuration and required scopes
workarounds.how: Ensure the server interceptor expects the same token format and scopes that the client is sending
